subject: hwmon: (lm80) fix a missing check of the status of SMBus read

features.post_operation: check return value < 0 then return error
features.critical_variable: return value of lm80_read_value (assigned to local int rv)
features.path_conditions: Any callsite where the returned int is consumed (e.g. arithmetic/bitwise composition into a register value) without first comparing to < 0 and propagating the error.
features.fit_test_reasoning: Classic missing-check pattern: lm80_read_value() returns an int that is negative on SMBus failure but the buggy code embeds it directly into a bitwise expression. The fix introduces an rv intermediate, a `if (rv < 0) return rv` guard, and only then uses rv. Fits the (failable_call, error_check_guard, unsafe_use) decomposition naturally; not a resource-lifecycle pattern but a return-status-check pattern.

plan_summary: 4 predicates: isFailableReadCall (name-matched smbus/i2c/read_value helpers returning int) -> returnValueUsedUnsafely (call result feeds arithmetic/bitwise op or assignment-then-arith use) -> hasErrorCheckGuard (some `<0` comparison on call result or variable receiving it exists in the function) -> uncheckedFailableRead (combine: failable & unsafe-use & no guard).
